ocr.text: In the analysis of site-specific class association rules, the table presents a comprehensive overview of various methods
employed to derive insights from the data. The metrics of Support and Confidence are particularly critical in evaluating the
strength of these association rules. Support indicates the proportion of instances in the dataset that meet the criteria of the rule,
while Confidence reflects the reliability of the inference made by the rule based on the data available. For example, the Wi-Fi
method demonstrates a Support metric of 92.6%, suggesting a strong correlation between the variables analyzed.
Method  Support  Confidence
Site-specific class Association rules
Wildtype 2378T in NR subtype 1b  50%  69%
A2368T in R in subtype 1a  100%  52.2%
E2356G/D in R in subtype 1b  76.3%  67.3%
Number of variable sitesThree variable sites in R  100%  25%
Six variable sites in R  1.7%  100%
Genetic distancesGD > 0.2 for the V3 region in R  55%  35%
Profile Hidden Markov ModelScore > 45 for R  70%  20%
Prediction  Wi-Fi  LTE  DVB-T  Noise  Unknown
Actual
Unknown  0%  0%  0%  0%  0%
This high Support value indicates that the rule applies to a significant portion of the dataset, enhancing its relevance for
practical applications. Additionally, the Confidence level of 1.85% indicates the likelihood that the conclusion drawn from this
rule will hold true when the rule is applied in similar contexts. Such metrics are essential for practitioners aiming to leverage
these rules for decision-making processes, as they provide a quantitative basis for understanding the relationships within the
data. Overall, the insights derived from these association rules can significantly contribute to optimizing site-specific strategies
and improving operational efficiencies.
